text.data-narration: Framing is the process of dividing data into manageable units called frames. Each frame consists of a header, payload, and trailer. The header contains control information such as destination MAC address, source MAC address, frame source, and frame type. The payload is the actual data being transmitted, and the trailer contains error-checking information.

g.text: Frame format: Destination MAC address, source MAC address, frame source, frame type, payload, trailer

text.data-narration: Flow control is a mechanism that prevents network congestion by regulating the amount of data transmitted by a device. It ensures that the sender and receiver agree on the amount of data to be transmitted before sending it. There are two types of flow control: window-based flow control and sliding window flow control.

g.text: Window size: The maximum amount of data that can be transmitted at one time. Acknowledgments: The receiver acknowledges the packets it receives.

text.data-narration: Error control is the process of detecting and correcting errors that occur during data transmission. This ensures that the data is delivered correctly and without errors.

g.text: Error-checking information: The trailer contains error-checking information to detect and correct errors.

text.data-narration: Connectionless protocols, such as UDP, do not guarantee delivery of data packets, while connection-oriented protocols, such as TCP, ensure reliable delivery of data packets.

g.text: TCP: Connection-oriented protocol that ensures reliable delivery of data packets.
